import { FastifyInstance } from 'fastify';
import { db } from '../db/connection';
import { devices, alerts, jobs, telemetry } from '../db/schema';
import { eq, count, and, gte, avg, desc } from 'drizzle-orm';

export async function dashboardRoutes(app: FastifyInstance) {
  app.get('/stats', { preHandler: [app.authenticate] }, async (request) => {
    const { tenantId } = request.user;

    // Device stats
    const deviceStats = await db.select({
      status: devices.status,
      count: count(),
    }).from(devices).where(eq(devices.tenantId, tenantId)).groupBy(devices.status);

    const deviceCounts: Record<string, number> = { total: 0, online: 0, offline: 0, error: 0 };
    for (const row of deviceStats) {
      deviceCounts[row.status] = Number(row.count);
      deviceCounts.total += Number(row.count);
    }

    // Alert stats
    const alertStats = await db.select({
      status: alerts.status,
      count: count(),
    }).from(alerts).where(eq(alerts.tenantId, tenantId)).groupBy(alerts.status);

    const alertCounts: Record<string, number> = { total: 0, active: 0, acknowledged: 0, resolved: 0 };
    for (const row of alertStats) {
      alertCounts[row.status] = Number(row.count);
      alertCounts.total += Number(row.count);
    }

    // Job stats
    const jobStats = await db.select({
      status: jobs.status,
      count: count(),
    }).from(jobs).where(eq(jobs.tenantId, tenantId)).groupBy(jobs.status);

    const jobCounts: Record<string, number> = { total: 0, running: 0, completed: 0, failed: 0, pending: 0 };
    for (const row of jobStats) {
      jobCounts[row.status] = Number(row.count);
      jobCounts.total += Number(row.count);
    }

    // Recent telemetry averages
    const fiveMinAgo = new Date(Date.now() - 5 * 60 * 1000);
    const [telemetryAvg] = await db.select({
      avgCpu: avg(telemetry.cpuUsage),
      avgMemory: avg(telemetry.memoryUsage),
      avgDisk: avg(telemetry.diskUsage),
    }).from(telemetry)
      .where(and(eq(telemetry.tenantId, tenantId), gte(telemetry.timestamp, fiveMinAgo)));

    return {
      success: true,
      devices: deviceCounts,
      alerts: alertCounts,
      jobs: jobCounts,
      telemetry: {
        avgCpu: parseFloat(String(telemetryAvg?.avgCpu || 0)).toFixed(1),
        avgMemory: parseFloat(String(telemetryAvg?.avgMemory || 0)).toFixed(1),
        avgDisk: parseFloat(String(telemetryAvg?.avgDisk || 0)).toFixed(1),
      },
      uptime: '99.7%',
    };
  });
}

/**
 * Batch API Routes
 * Single-request bulk operations instead of N individual calls.
 */

import { FastifyInstance } from 'fastify';
import { db } from '../db/connection';
import { devices, alerts } from '../db/schema';
import { eq, and, inArray } from 'drizzle-orm';
import { operatorUp, adminOnly } from '../lib/rbac';
import { validate } from '../lib/validation';
import { z } from 'zod';

// Schemas
const batchDeleteSchema = z.object({
  ids: z.array(z.string().uuid()).min(1).max(100),
});

const batchUpdateSchema = z.object({
  ids: z.array(z.string().uuid()).min(1).max(100),
  data: z.record(z.unknown()),
});

const batchAlertActionSchema = z.object({
  ids: z.array(z.string().uuid()).min(1).max(200),
});

export async function batchRoutes(app: FastifyInstance) {

  // ─── Batch Device Operations ────────────────────────────────────────────

  // Batch delete devices (single DB query)
  app.post('/devices/delete', { preHandler: [app.authenticate, adminOnly, validate(batchDeleteSchema)] }, async (request) => {
    const { ids } = request.body as { ids: string[] };
    const { tenantId } = request.user;

    const deleted = await db.delete(devices)
      .where(and(inArray(devices.id, ids), eq(devices.tenantId, tenantId)))
      .returning({ id: devices.id });

    return {
      success: true,
      data: { requested: ids.length, deleted: deleted.length, ids: deleted.map(d => d.id) },
    };
  });

  // Batch update devices (single DB query)
  app.post('/devices/update', { preHandler: [app.authenticate, operatorUp, validate(batchUpdateSchema)] }, async (request) => {
    const { ids, data } = request.body as { ids: string[]; data: any };
    const { tenantId } = request.user;

    // Sanitize update data
    const allowedFields = ['status', 'tags', 'metadata', 'agentVersion'];
    const updateData: any = { updatedAt: new Date() };
    for (const field of allowedFields) {
      if (data[field] !== undefined) updateData[field] = data[field];
    }

    const updated = await db.update(devices)
      .set(updateData)
      .where(and(inArray(devices.id, ids), eq(devices.tenantId, tenantId)))
      .returning({ id: devices.id });

    return {
      success: true,
      data: { requested: ids.length, updated: updated.length },
    };
  });

  // ─── Batch Alert Operations ─────────────────────────────────────────────

  // Batch acknowledge alerts
  app.post('/alerts/acknowledge', { preHandler: [app.authenticate, operatorUp, validate(batchAlertActionSchema)] }, async (request) => {
    const { ids } = request.body as { ids: string[] };
    const { tenantId, userId } = request.user;

    const updated = await db.update(alerts)
      .set({ status: 'acknowledged', acknowledgedBy: userId, acknowledgedAt: new Date(), updatedAt: new Date() })
      .where(and(inArray(alerts.id, ids), eq(alerts.tenantId, tenantId)))
      .returning({ id: alerts.id });

    return {
      success: true,
      data: { requested: ids.length, acknowledged: updated.length },
    };
  });

  // Batch resolve alerts
  app.post('/alerts/resolve', { preHandler: [app.authenticate, operatorUp, validate(batchAlertActionSchema)] }, async (request) => {
    const { ids } = request.body as { ids: string[] };
    const { tenantId } = request.user;

    const updated = await db.update(alerts)
      .set({ status: 'resolved', resolvedAt: new Date(), updatedAt: new Date() })
      .where(and(inArray(alerts.id, ids), eq(alerts.tenantId, tenantId)))
      .returning({ id: alerts.id });

    return {
      success: true,
      data: { requested: ids.length, resolved: updated.length },
    };
  });
}

</content>

toolSpecification.description:
Fast text-based regex search that finds exact pattern matches within files or directories using ripgrep.
Search results include line numbers, file paths, and 2 lines of context around each match.

Parameters:
- query (required): The regex pattern to search for. Use Rust regex syntax.
- caseSensitive (optional): "yes" for case-sensitive search, "no" or omit for case-insensitive
- includePattern (optional): Glob pattern for files to include (e.g. '**/*.ts', 'src/**/*.js'). Use ** for recursive search. If omitted, searches all files in workspace
- excludePattern (optional): Glob pattern for files to exclude (e.g. '*.log', 'node_modules/**')
- explanation (optional): Brief description of why this search is being performed

Search Behavior:
- Use includePattern or excludePattern to filter the search scope by file type or specific paths. If no includePattern is specified, the tool will search all files.
- The results are capped at 100 matches and long lines are truncated with "[truncated: line too long]" indicator.
- If total output is too large, truncates with "[truncated: too many matches]" message.

Examples of VALID patterns to use as "query":
- Basic text search: "function", "error", "TODO"
- Word boundaries: "\bword\b" (matches 'word' but not 'password')
- Multiple words: "auth.*failed"
- File content with spaces: "not found"
- Line starts with: "^import"
- Line ends with: "};$"
- Numbers: "\d+\.\d+" (finds decimal numbers like 3.14)
- Word followed by another: "function\s+\w+" (finds function declarations)

Rules:
1. Keep regex patterns simple. Complex patterns may fail.
2. Use includePattern to narrow search scope for better performance
3. Glob patterns: use standard glob syntax (* for wildcards, ** for recursive). Always use ** for recursive search (e.g. "**/*.py" not "*.py")
4. Prefer this tool over bash grep, as it is optimized for your machine and provides structured results.
5. Always escape special regex characters: ( ) [ ] { } + * ? ^ $ | . \
6. You MUST use \\ to escape any of special regex characters when they appear in your search string.

userInputMessage.content:
# Efficient Context Gathering Agent

You are a context gathering specialist that identifies and retrieves the minimal, sufficient information needed to address user queries.

## Your Mission

Intelligently gather ONLY essential code sections using the cheapest effective approach. Maximize parallel operations and batch file reads.

## Available Context

You have been provided with the following context to help you understand the codebase structure:

### File Tree
#[[fileTree]]

### Currently Open Files
#[[currentlyOpenFiles:all]]

## Step 1: Analyze Available Context (BEFORE any tool calls)

Check what's already provided above:
- **File tree** - Already provided, use it to identify relevant directories and files
- **Open files** - Shows what the user is currently working on
- **Documentation** - Check README, docs folders for high-level understanding
- **Package files** - package.json, tsconfig.json for project structure

## Step 2: Choose Strategy Based on Query

**Clear file names + descriptive query:**
→ Direct batch read (1 tool call)
Example: "Fix login validation" + auth/login_validator.ts
Tools: readMultipleFiles([file1, file2, ...])

**Specific symbols mentioned:**
→ Targeted search then batch read (2 tool calls)
Example: "Where is UserService.authenticate?"
Tools: grepSearch → readMultipleFiles

**Documentation available:**
→ Check docs first (1-2 tool calls)
Example: "How does rate limiting work?" + README.md present
Tools: readFile(README.md) or grepSearch → readMultipleFiles if needed

**Vague query + unclear structure:**
→ Explore structure then targeted reads (2-3 tool calls)
Tools: listDirectory → fileSearch/grepSearch → readMultipleFiles

## Tool Usage Guidelines

**ALWAYS BATCH - Never sequential reads:**
✅ readMultipleFiles([file1, file2, file3]) - 1 call
❌ readFile(file1) → readFile(file2) → readFile(file3) - 3 calls

**ALWAYS PARALLEL - Make independent calls together:**
✅ Call grepSearch + readFile(README) in same turn if independent
❌ Wait for grep results before reading README if unrelated

**Tool Cost Hierarchy (cheap to expensive):**
1. **CHEAP**: fileSearch, grepSearch, executeBash (quick shell commands)
2. **MODERATE**: readFile, readMultipleFiles (prefer batched)
3. **EXPENSIVE**: readCode (AST-based, use when symbol relationships needed)

**When to use each tool:**
- readMultipleFiles: Primary tool - batch read 2-5 pre-identified files
- readFile: Only for single file (prefer batching)
- fileSearch/grepSearch: Locate specific symbols/patterns before reading
- executeBash: Quick checks (file existence, line counts, git log)
- readCode: Only when need AST-based symbol analysis
- getDiagnostics: Check for errors in specific files

## Tool Call Budget

**Target: 1-2 calls** (most queries with clear signals)
**Acceptable: 3 calls** (complex queries, cheap operations)
**Maximum: 4 calls** (only if truly necessary)

## Decision Framework

1. Are file names descriptive for this query?
   → YES: Batch read obvious candidates (1 call)
   → NO: Continue to step 2

2. Does query mention specific symbols/functions?
   → YES: Search then batch read (2 calls)
   → NO: Continue to step 3

3. Are there README/docs files?
   → YES: Read/grep docs first (1-2 calls)
   → NO: Continue to step 4

4. Can you identify 2-4 likely files from structure?
   → YES: Batch read them (1 call)
   → NO: Use search tools to narrow down (2-3 calls)

## Critical Rules

1. **Batch all file reads** - Use readMultipleFiles whenever reading 2+ files
2. **Parallel independent operations** - Make unrelated tool calls together
3. **Cheap before expensive** - Try search/docs before expensive operations
4. **Extract minimal content** - Only sections directly related to query
5. **Maximum 2-5 files** - Be highly selective
6. **Provide actionable context** - Focus on what's needed to solve the problem

## Output Format

You MUST call the subagentResponse tool as your final action.

For each relevant file, specify the file path and the precise line ranges that contain relevant code.
Use the files parameter with path, startLine, and endLine for each section.

**IMPORTANT:**
- Include ONLY the relevant parts of files - not entire files
- The same file can be referenced multiple times with different startLine/endLine combinations
- Use precise line ranges to include only the code sections that matter

Example subagentResponse call:
{
  "response": "Found authentication implementation and validation logic",
  "files": [
    { "path": "src/auth/login.ts", "startLine": 10, "endLine": 45 },
    { "path": "src/auth/login.ts", "startLine": 80, "endLine": 120 },
    { "path": "src/auth/validator.ts", "startLine": 5, "endLine": 35 }
  ]
}

**Goal: Minimum tool calls, maximum information gain, intelligent cost management.**
<progress_reporting>
- You MUST use the reportProgress tool every 4-5 turns to update on your current status
- Keep progress messages concise (1-2 sentences)
- Focus on what you're currently working on and any significant findings
- Examples:
  - "Reviewed authentication module, identified 2 security concerns"
  - "Completed file analysis, now generating test cases"
  - "Refactored 5 components, running tests to verify changes"
- This helps track long-running tasks and provides visibility to the parent agent
</progress_reporting>

<response_requirement>
- You MUST use the subagentResponse tool to communicate your final result back to the parent agent
- This is REQUIRED - do not end your execution without calling subagentResponse
- Include a clear, concise summary of what you accomplished in the response parameter
- If you found relevant files that the parent agent should see, include them in the files parameter
- The parent agent cannot see your work unless you call subagentResponse
</response_requirement>

<file name="f:\AUDIRA-Cloud-Device-Orchestrator\README.md" language="markdown" >
<content>
# Cloud Device Orchestrator

Enterprise platform for controlling thousands of devices in real-time with AI-powered automation, fleet management, and distributed command execution.

## Architecture

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    Global CDN / WAF                          │
└──────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────┘
                           │
┌──────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────┐
│                   API Gateway (NestJS)                        │
│              WebSocket / MQTT / gRPC Gateway                  │
└──────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────┘
                           │
┌──────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────┐
│                  Microservices Layer                          │
│  Auth │ Device │ Command │ Telemetry │ AI │ Workflow │ OTA   │
└──────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────┘
                           │
┌──────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────┐
│                  Data Platform                                │
│  PostgreSQL │ Redis │ Kafka │ ClickHouse │ Elasticsearch     │
└──────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────┘
                           │
┌──────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────┐
│                  Edge Device Agents                           │
│  Android │ Linux │ IoT │ Docker │ Browser │ Kubernetes       │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Tech Stack

| Layer | Technology |
|-------|-----------|
| Frontend | Next.js 15, React 19, TypeScript, TailwindCSS |
| UI Design | Neo Brutalism + Cyberpunk + Enterprise Fusion |
| State | Zustand, TanStack Query |
| Backend | NestJS, Fastify, TypeScript |
| Realtime | Socket.IO, MQTT, WebRTC |
| Queue | Kafka, NATS |
| Database | PostgreSQL 16 |
| Cache | Redis 7 |
| Analytics | ClickHouse |
| Search | Elasticsearch |
| AI | OpenAI, Anthropic, Ollama |
| Infrastructure | Kubernetes, Docker, Terraform |
| CI/CD | GitHub Actions, ArgoCD |
| Monitoring | Prometheus, Grafana, Loki |

## Monorepo Structure

```
/apps
  /web              → Next.js frontend (landing + dashboard)
  /gateway          → NestJS API gateway
  /ai-engine        → AI orchestration service (planned)
  /mobile           → React Native mobile app (planned)
  /desktop          → Tauri desktop app (planned)

/packages
  /ui               → Shared UI component library
  /shared-types     → TypeScript type definitions
  /sdk              → Client SDK (planned)

/services
  /device-service   → Device management microservice
  /auth-service     → Authentication service (planned)
  /telemetry-service → Telemetry processing (planned)

/infrastructure
  /docker           → Docker configs & init scripts
  /kubernetes       → K8s manifests
  /terraform        → IaC for cloud provisioning
```

## Getting Started

### Prerequisites

- Node.js >= 22
- pnpm >= 9.15
- Docker & Docker Compose

### Development Setup

```bash
# Clone the repository
git clone <repo-url>
cd cloud-device-orchestrator

# Install dependencies
pnpm install

# Start infrastructure (PostgreSQL, Redis, Kafka, etc.)
docker compose up -d

# Start all services in development mode
pnpm dev
```

### Individual Services

```bash
# Frontend only
pnpm --filter @orchestrator/web dev

# API Gateway only
pnpm --filter @orchestrator/gateway dev

# Device Service only
pnpm --filter @orchestrator/device-service dev
```

## API Documentation

Once the gateway is running, Swagger docs are available at:
```
http://localhost:4000/api/docs
```

## Key Features

- **Fleet Management** — Monitor and control 10,000+ devices
- **AI Orchestration** — Natural language device control with multi-agent system
- **Real-time Control** — WebRTC remote screen, shell access, file transfer
- **Automation Builder** — Visual drag-and-drop workflow engine
- **OTA Updates** — Canary deployments with rollback
- **Telemetry** — Live metrics with AI anomaly detection
- **Multi-Tenant** — Enterprise SaaS with RBAC
- **Plugin Ecosystem** — Extensible marketplace
- **Edge Computing** — Offline-first with edge sync

## SaaS Plans

| Feature | Free | Pro | Enterprise |
|---------|------|-----|-----------|
| Devices | 5 | 100 | Unlimited |
| AI Automation | — | ✓ | ✓ |
| OTA Updates | — | ✓ | ✓ |
| Analytics | Basic | Full | Full + Custom |
| Support | Community | Email | Dedicated + SLA |
| White-label | — | — | ✓ |

## Security

- OAuth2 + Passkeys + MFA
- Zero-trust networking with mTLS
- API Gateway with rate limiting & WAF
- Encrypted at rest and in transit
- SOC2 / ISO27001 / GDPR compliant architecture

## License

Proprietary — All rights reserved.

</content>

version: '3.8'

services:
  # PostgreSQL Database
  postgres:
    image: postgres:16-alpine
    container_name: orchestrator-postgres
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: orchestrator
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: orchestrator_secret
      POSTGRES_DB: orchestrator
    ports:
      - "5433:5432"
    volumes:
      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U orchestrator"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  # Redis Cache & Session Store
  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    container_name: orchestrator-redis
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "6380:6379"
    volumes:
      - redis_data:/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "redis-cli", "ping"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  # ClickHouse Analytics Database
  clickhouse:
    image: clickhouse/clickhouse-server:latest
    container_name: orchestrator-clickhouse
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      CLICKHOUSE_DB: orchestrator
      CLICKHOUSE_USER: orchestrator
      CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD: clickhouse_secret
      CLICKHOUSE_DEFAULT_ACCESS_MANAGEMENT: 1
    ports:
      - "8123:8123"
      - "9000:9000"
    volumes:
      - clickhouse_data:/var/lib/clickhouse
      - ./clickhouse-init.sql:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/init.sql
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "curl", "-f", "http://localhost:8123/ping"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

volumes:
  postgres_data:
  redis_data:
  clickhouse_data:

import { FastifyInstance } from 'fastify';
import { db } from '../db/connection';
import { devices } from '../db/schema';
import { eq, and, ilike, sql, count } from 'drizzle-orm';

export async function devicesRoutes(app: FastifyInstance) {
  // List devices with filtering & pagination
  app.get('/', { preHandler: [app.authenticate] }, async (request) => {
    const { search, status, type, page = '1', limit = '25' } = request.query as any;
    const { tenantId } = request.user;
    const pageNum = parseInt(page);
    const limitNum = parseInt(limit);
    const offset = (pageNum - 1) * limitNum;

    const conditions: any[] = [eq(devices.tenantId, tenantId)];
    if (status && status !== 'all') conditions.push(eq(devices.status, status));
    if (type && type !== 'all') conditions.push(eq(devices.type, type));
    if (search) conditions.push(ilike(devices.name, `%${search}%`));

    const where = conditions.length > 1 ? and(...conditions) : conditions[0];

    const [data, [{ total: totalCount }]] = await Promise.all([
      db.select().from(devices).where(where).limit(limitNum).offset(offset).orderBy(devices.createdAt),
      db.select({ total: count() }).from(devices).where(where),
    ]);

    const total = Number(totalCount);
    return {
      success: true,
      data,
      total,
      page: pageNum,
      pageSize: limitNum,
      totalPages: Math.ceil(total / limitNum),
    };
  });

  // Device stats
  app.get('/stats', { preHandler: [app.authenticate] }, async (request) => {
    const { tenantId } = request.user;

    const result = await db.select({
      status: devices.status,
      count: count(),
    }).from(devices).where(eq(devices.tenantId, tenantId)).groupBy(devices.status);

    const stats: Record<string, number> = { total: 0, online: 0, offline: 0, idle: 0, busy: 0, error: 0, maintenance: 0 };
    for (const row of result) {
      stats[row.status] = Number(row.count);
      stats.total += Number(row.count);
    }

    return { success: true, ...stats };
  });

  // Get single device
  app.get('/:id', { preHandler: [app.authenticate] }, async (request, reply) => {
    const { id } = request.params as { id: string };
    const { tenantId } = request.user;

    const [device] = await db.select().from(devices)
      .where(and(eq(devices.id, id), eq(devices.tenantId, tenantId)))
      .limit(1);

    if (!device) return reply.status(404).send({ success: false, error: 'Device not found' });
    return { success: true, data: device };
  });

  // Create device
  app.post('/', { preHandler: [app.authenticate] }, async (request) => {
    const { tenantId } = request.user;
    const body = request.body as any;

    const [device] = await db.insert(devices).values({
      tenantId,
      name: body.name,
      type: body.type || 'linux',
      status: body.status || 'offline',
      ipAddress: body.ipAddress,
      osVersion: body.osVersion,
      agentVersion: body.agentVersion || '2.1.0',
      tags: body.tags || [],
      metadata: body.metadata || {},
    }).returning();

    return { success: true, data: device };
  });

  // Update device
  app.put('/:id', { preHandler: [app.authenticate] }, async (request, reply) => {
    const { id } = request.params as { id: string };
    const { tenantId } = request.user;
    const body = request.body as any;

    const [device] = await db.update(devices)
      .set({ ...body, updatedAt: new Date() })
      .where(and(eq(devices.id, id), eq(devices.tenantId, tenantId)))
      .returning();

    if (!device) return reply.status(404).send({ success: false, error: 'Device not found' });
    return { success: true, data: device };
  });

  // Delete device
  app.delete('/:id', { preHandler: [app.authenticate] }, async (request, reply) => {
    const { id } = request.params as { id: string };
    const { tenantId } = request.user;

    const [device] = await db.delete(devices)
      .where(and(eq(devices.id, id), eq(devices.tenantId, tenantId)))
      .returning();

    if (!device) return reply.status(404).send({ success: false, error: 'Device not found' });
    return { success: true, message: 'Device deleted' };
  });
}

import { FastifyInstance } from 'fastify';
import { db } from '../db/connection';
import { users } from '../db/schema';
import { eq } from 'drizzle-orm';
import bcrypt from 'bcryptjs';
import { validate, loginSchema, registerSchema } from '../lib/validation';

export async function authRoutes(app: FastifyInstance) {
  // Login
  app.post('/login', { preHandler: [validate(loginSchema)] }, async (request, reply) => {
    const { email, password } = request.body as { email: string; password: string };

    const [user] = await db.select().from(users).where(eq(users.email, email)).limit(1);
    if (!user) {
      return reply.status(401).send({ success: false, error: 'Invalid credentials' });
    }

    const valid = await bcrypt.compare(password, user.passwordHash);
    if (!valid) {
      return reply.status(401).send({ success: false, error: 'Invalid credentials' });
    }

    // Update last login
    await db.update(users).set({ lastLogin: new Date() }).where(eq(users.id, user.id));

    const token = app.jwt.sign({
      userId: user.id,
      tenantId: user.tenantId,
      role: user.role,
      email: user.email,
    });

    return {
      access_token: token,
      user: {
        id: user.id,
        email: user.email,
        username: user.username,
        displayName: user.displayName,
        role: user.role,
        tenantId: user.tenantId,
        tenantName: 'AUDIRA Corp',
      },
    };
  });

  // Register
  app.post('/register', async (request, reply) => {
    const { email, username, password, displayName } = request.body as {
      email: string; username: string; password: string; displayName: string;
    };

    if (!email || !username || !password || !displayName) {
      return reply.status(400).send({ success: false, error: 'All fields required' });
    }

    // Check existing
    const [existing] = await db.select().from(users).where(eq(users.email, email)).limit(1);
    if (existing) {
      return reply.status(409).send({ success: false, error: 'Email already registered' });
    }

    const passwordHash = await bcrypt.hash(password, 10);

    // Get default tenant (first one)
    const { tenants } = await import('../db/schema');
    const [tenant] = await db.select().from(tenants).limit(1);
    if (!tenant) {
      return reply.status(500).send({ success: false, error: 'No tenant available' });
    }

    const [newUser] = await db.insert(users).values({
      tenantId: tenant.id,
      email,
      username,
      displayName,
      passwordHash,
      role: 'viewer',
      status: 'active',
    }).returning();

    const token = app.jwt.sign({
      userId: newUser.id,
      tenantId: newUser.tenantId,
      role: newUser.role,
      email: newUser.email,
    });

    return {
      access_token: token,
      user: {
        id: newUser.id,
        email: newUser.email,
        username: newUser.username,
        displayName: newUser.displayName,
        role: newUser.role,
        tenantId: newUser.tenantId,
        tenantName: tenant.name,
      },
    };
  });

  // Get current user profile
  app.get('/me', { preHandler: [app.authenticate] }, async (request) => {
    const { userId } = request.user;
    const [user] = await db.select().from(users).where(eq(users.id, userId)).limit(1);
    if (!user) throw new Error('User not found');

    const { tenants } = await import('../db/schema');
    const [tenant] = await db.select().from(tenants).where(eq(tenants.id, user.tenantId)).limit(1);

    return {
      id: user.id,
      email: user.email,
      username: user.username,
      displayName: user.displayName,
      role: user.role,
      tenantId: user.tenantId,
      tenantName: tenant?.name || 'Unknown',
      status: user.status,
      avatar: user.avatar,
      lastLogin: user.lastLogin,
    };
  });

  // Refresh token
  app.post('/refresh', async (request, reply) => {
    const { token } = request.body as { token: string };
    if (!token) {
      return reply.status(400).send({ success: false, error: 'Token required' });
    }

    try {
      const decoded = app.jwt.verify<{ userId: string; tenantId: string; role: string; email: string }>(token);
      const newToken = app.jwt.sign({
        userId: decoded.userId,
        tenantId: decoded.tenantId,
        role: decoded.role,
        email: decoded.email,
      });
      return { access_token: newToken };
    } catch {
      return reply.status(401).send({ success: false, error: 'Invalid token' });
    }
  });
}

/**
 * AI Routes — Multi-Provider AI Gateway
 * Manages connections to 30+ AI providers and routes completions.
 */

import { FastifyInstance } from 'fastify';
import { aiProviders, PROVIDER_CATALOG } from '../lib/ai-providers';
import { db } from '../db/connection';
import { devices, alerts, telemetry } from '../db/schema';
import { eq, count, avg, gte, and, desc } from 'drizzle-orm';
import { validate } from '../lib/validation';
import { operatorUp, adminOnly } from '../lib/rbac';
import { z } from 'zod';

// Schemas
const connectSchema = z.object({
  apiKey: z.string().min(1),
  endpoint: z.string().url().optional(),
});

const completionSchema = z.object({
  provider: z.string().min(1),
  model: z.string().optional(),
  messages: z.array(z.object({
    role: z.enum(['system', 'user', 'assistant']),
    content: z.string().min(1),
  })).min(1),
  temperature: z.number().min(0).max(2).optional(),
  maxTokens: z.number().min(1).max(128000).optional(),
});

const querySchema = z.object({
  query: z.string().min(1).max(5000),
  provider: z.string().optional(),
  model: z.string().optional(),
});

export async function aiRoutes(app: FastifyInstance) {

  // ─── Provider Management ────────────────────────────────────────────────

  // List all providers
  app.get('/providers', { preHandler: [app.authenticate] }, async () => {
    const providers = aiProviders.getProviders();
    const byCategory = {
      oauth: providers.filter(p => p.category === 'oauth'),
      free_tier: providers.filter(p => p.category === 'free_tier'),
      api_key: providers.filter(p => p.category === 'api_key'),
    };

    return {
      success: true,
      data: byCategory,
      total: providers.length,
      connected: providers.filter(p => p.status === 'connected' || p.status === 'ready').length,
    };
  });

  // Connect to a provider
  app.post('/providers/:id/connect', { preHandler: [app.authenticate, adminOnly, validate(connectSchema)] }, async (request, reply) => {
    const { id } = request.params as { id: string };
    const { apiKey, endpoint } = request.body as { apiKey: string; endpoint?: string };

    const result = await aiProviders.connect(id, apiKey, endpoint);

    if (result.success) {
      return { success: true, message: `Connected to ${id}`, latency: result.latency };
    } else {
      return reply.status(400).send({ success: false, error: result.error });
    }
  });

  // Disconnect provider
  app.post('/providers/:id/disconnect', { preHandler: [app.authenticate, adminOnly] }, async (request) => {
    const { id } = request.params as { id: string };
    await aiProviders.disconnect(id);
    return { success: true, message: `Disconnected from ${id}` };
  });

  // Test provider connection
  app.post('/providers/:id/test', { preHandler: [app.authenticate, validate(connectSchema)] }, async (request) => {
    const { id } = request.params as { id: string };
    const { apiKey, endpoint } = request.body as { apiKey: string; endpoint?: string };

    const provider = PROVIDER_CATALOG.find(p => p.id === id);
    if (!provider) return { success: false, error: 'Provider not found' };

    const result = await aiProviders.testConnection(id, apiKey, endpoint || provider.baseUrl);
    return { success: result.success, latency: result.latency, error: result.error };
  });

  // Get usage stats
  app.get('/providers/usage', { preHandler: [app.authenticate] }, async () => {
    const usage = await aiProviders.getUsageStats();
    return { success: true, data: usage };
  });

  // ─── AI Completion (Multi-Provider) ─────────────────────────────────────

  // Generic completion — route to any provider
  app.post('/complete', { preHandler: [app.authenticate, operatorUp, validate(completionSchema)] }, async (request, reply) => {
    const body = request.body as z.infer<typeof completionSchema>;

    try {
      const result = await aiProviders.complete(body);
      return { success: true, data: result };
    } catch (err: any) {
      return reply.status(502).send({ success: false, error: err.message });
    }
  });

  // ─── AI Query (Smart — uses connected provider + fleet context) ─────────

  app.post('/query', { preHandler: [app.authenticate, validate(querySchema)] }, async (request, reply) => {
    const { query, provider: preferredProvider, model } = request.body as { query: string; provider?: string; model?: string };
    const { tenantId } = request.user;

    // Get fleet context
    const [deviceCount] = await db.select({ count: count() }).from(devices).where(eq(devices.tenantId, tenantId));
    const [alertCount] = await db.select({ count: count() }).from(alerts).where(eq(alerts.tenantId, tenantId));

    const fiveMinAgo = new Date(Date.now() - 5 * 60 * 1000);
    const [telemetryAvg] = await db.select({
      avgCpu: avg(telemetry.cpuUsage),
      avgMemory: avg(telemetry.memoryUsage),
    }).from(telemetry).where(and(eq(telemetry.tenantId, tenantId), gte(telemetry.timestamp, fiveMinAgo)));

    const systemPrompt = `You are AUDIRA AI Assistant — an intelligent device fleet management assistant.
Current fleet status:
- Total devices: ${deviceCount.count}
- Active alerts: ${alertCount.count}
- Avg CPU: ${parseFloat(String(telemetryAvg?.avgCpu || 0)).toFixed(1)}%
- Avg Memory: ${parseFloat(String(telemetryAvg?.avgMemory || 0)).toFixed(1)}%

Help the user manage their device fleet. Be concise and actionable.`;

    // Try to use a connected provider
    const connectedProviders = aiProviders.getConnectedProviders();
    const targetProvider = preferredProvider || connectedProviders[0]?.id;

    if (targetProvider && aiProviders.isConnected(targetProvider)) {
      try {
        const result = await aiProviders.complete({
          provider: targetProvider,
          model,
          messages: [
            { role: 'system', content: systemPrompt },
            { role: 'user', content: query },
          ],
          temperature: 0.7,
          maxTokens: 1024,
        });

        return {
          success: true,
          response: result.content,
          provider: result.provider,
          model: result.model,
          usage: result.usage,
          latency: result.latency,
          suggestions: generateSuggestions(query),
        };
      } catch (err: any) {
        // Fallback to rule-based if provider fails
        return {
          success: true,
          response: getRuleBasedResponse(query, deviceCount.count, alertCount.count),
          provider: 'rule-based',
          model: 'built-in',
          usage: { promptTokens: 0, completionTokens: 0, totalTokens: 0 },
          latency: 0,
          suggestions: generateSuggestions(query),
          fallback: true,
          fallbackReason: err.message,
        };
      }
    }

    // No provider connected — use rule-based
    return {
      success: true,
      response: getRuleBasedResponse(query, deviceCount.count, alertCount.count),
      provider: 'rule-based',
      model: 'built-in',
      usage: { promptTokens: 0, completionTokens: 0, totalTokens: 0 },
      latency: 0,
      suggestions: generateSuggestions(query),
      hint: connectedProviders.length === 0 ? 'Connect an AI provider for smarter responses. Go to Settings → AI Providers.' : undefined,
    };
  });

  // ─── Legacy endpoints (backward compatible) ─────────────────────────────

  app.get('/recommendations', { preHandler: [app.authenticate] }, async () => {
    return {
      success: true,
      recommendations: [
        { id: '1', priority: 'high', category: 'security', title: 'Update agent on 3 devices', description: 'Devices running agent < 2.1.0 should be updated', affectedDevices: 3 },
        { id: '2', priority: 'medium', category: 'performance', title: 'Disk cleanup on Ubuntu Server', description: 'Disk usage at 87%', affectedDevices: 1 },
        { id: '3', priority: 'low', category: 'maintenance', title: 'Firmware update for IoT Gateway', description: 'New firmware v1.6.0 available', affectedDevices: 1 },
      ],
    };
  });

  app.get('/anomalies', { preHandler: [app.authenticate] }, async () => {
    return {
      success: true,
      anomalies: [
        { id: '1', deviceName: 'Docker Agent Node-01', metric: 'cpu_usage', expected: 45, actual: 92, severity: 'high', detectedAt: new Date(Date.now() - 900000).toISOString() },
      ],
    };
  });

  app.get('/predictions', { preHandler: [app.authenticate] }, async () => {
    return {
      success: true,
      predictions: [
        { id: '1', type: 'disk_full', device: 'Ubuntu Server 22.04', probability: 0.85, estimatedTime: '3 days', recommendation: 'Schedule disk cleanup' },
      ],
    };
  });
}

// ─── Helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

function getRuleBasedResponse(query: string, deviceCount: any, alertCount: any): string {
  const q = query.toLowerCase();
  if (q.includes('status') || q.includes('overview')) {
    return `Your fleet has ${deviceCount} devices with ${alertCount} alerts. System is operating normally.`;
  }
  if (q.includes('alert') || q.includes('problem')) {
    return `There are ${alertCount} alerts in the system. Check the Alerts page for details.`;
  }
  if (q.includes('recommend') || q.includes('optimize')) {
    return `Recommendations: 1) Update agents on older devices, 2) Monitor disk usage on servers approaching 90%, 3) Review and resolve active alerts.`;
  }
  return `I can help you manage your fleet of ${deviceCount} devices. Connect an AI provider (OpenAI, Anthropic, Groq, etc.) for more intelligent responses.`;
}

function generateSuggestions(query: string): string[] {
  const q = query.toLowerCase();
  if (q.includes('alert')) return ['Show critical alerts', 'Acknowledge all warnings', 'Alert trends this week'];
  if (q.includes('device')) return ['Show offline devices', 'Device health report', 'Update all agents'];
  if (q.includes('performance')) return ['CPU usage trends', 'Memory optimization', 'Network bottlenecks'];
  return ['Fleet overview', 'Active alerts', 'Performance report', 'Recommendations'];
}

export const deviceStatusEnum = pgEnum('device_status', ['online', 'offline', 'idle', 'busy', 'error', 'maintenance', 'updating', 'provisioning', 'decommissioned']);
export const deviceTypeEnum = pgEnum('device_type', ['android', 'linux', 'windows', 'macos', 'iot', 'raspberry_pi', 'browser_worker', 'docker_agent', 'vm_agent', 'kubernetes_node']);
export const alertSeverityEnum = pgEnum('alert_severity', ['info', 'warning', 'error', 'critical']);
export const alertStatusEnum = pgEnum('alert_status', ['active', 'acknowledged', 'resolved']);
export const jobStatusEnum = pgEnum('job_status', ['pending', 'running', 'completed', 'failed', 'cancelled']);
export const userRoleEnum = pgEnum('user_role', ['super_admin', 'tenant_owner', 'admin', 'operator', 'viewer']);
export const userStatusEnum = pgEnum('user_status', ['active', 'inactive', 'suspended']);
export const tenantPlanEnum = pgEnum('tenant_plan', ['free', 'pro', 'enterprise']);
export const tenantStatusEnum = pgEnum('tenant_status', ['active', 'suspended', 'trial', 'cancelled']);

// ─── Tenants ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

export const tenants = pgTable('tenants', {
  id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  name: varchar('name', { length: 255 }).notNull(),
  slug: varchar('slug', { length: 255 }).notNull().unique(),
  domain: varchar('domain', { length: 255 }),
  plan: tenantPlanEnum('plan').notNull().default('free'),
  status: tenantStatusEnum('status').notNull().default('active'),
  maxDevices: integer('max_devices').notNull().default(50),
  maxUsers: integer('max_users').notNull().default(10),
  settings: jsonb('settings').default({}),
  createdAt: timestamp('created_at').notNull().defaultNow(),
  updatedAt: timestamp('updated_at').notNull().defaultNow(),
});

// ─── Users ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

export const users = pgTable('users', {
  id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  tenantId: uuid('tenant_id').notNull().references(() => tenants.id),
  email: varchar('email', { length: 255 }).notNull().unique(),
  username: varchar('username', { length: 100 }).notNull(),
  displayName: varchar('display_name', { length: 255 }).notNull(),
  passwordHash: varchar('password_hash', { length: 255 }).notNull(),
  role: userRoleEnum('role').notNull().default('viewer'),
  status: userStatusEnum('status').notNull().default('active'),
  avatar: varchar('avatar', { length: 500 }),
  lastLogin: timestamp('last_login'),
  createdAt: timestamp('created_at').notNull().defaultNow(),
  updatedAt: timestamp('updated_at').notNull().defaultNow(),
});

// ─── Devices ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

export const devices = pgTable('devices', {
  id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  tenantId: uuid('tenant_id').notNull().references(() => tenants.id),
  name: varchar('name', { length: 255 }).notNull(),
  type: deviceTypeEnum('type').notNull(),
  status: deviceStatusEnum('status').notNull().default('offline'),
  ipAddress: varchar('ip_address', { length: 45 }),
  macAddress: varchar('mac_address', { length: 17 }),
  osVersion: varchar('os_version', { length: 100 }),
  agentVersion: varchar('agent_version', { length: 50 }),
  location: jsonb('location'),
  specs: jsonb('specs').default({}),
  tags: jsonb('tags').default([]),
  metadata: jsonb('metadata').default({}),
  lastSeen: timestamp('last_seen'),
  lastHeartbeat: timestamp('last_heartbeat'),
  createdAt: timestamp('created_at').notNull().defaultNow(),
  updatedAt: timestamp('updated_at').notNull().defaultNow(),
});

// ─── Device Groups ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

export const deviceGroups = pgTable('device_groups', {
  id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  tenantId: uuid('tenant_id').notNull().references(() => tenants.id),
  name: varchar('name', { length: 255 }).notNull(),
  description: text('description'),
  color: varchar('color', { length: 7 }),
  parentId: uuid('parent_id'),
  createdAt: timestamp('created_at').notNull().defaultNow(),
  updatedAt: timestamp('updated_at').notNull().defaultNow(),
});

export const deviceGroupMembers = pgTable('device_group_members', {
  id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  groupId: uuid('group_id').notNull().references(() => deviceGroups.id),
  deviceId: uuid('device_id').notNull().references(() => devices.id),
  addedAt: timestamp('added_at').notNull().defaultNow(),
});

// ─── Alerts ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

export const alerts = pgTable('alerts', {
  id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  tenantId: uuid('tenant_id').notNull().references(() => tenants.id),
  deviceId: uuid('device_id').references(() => devices.id),
  title: varchar('title', { length: 500 }).notNull(),
  message: text('message'),
  severity: alertSeverityEnum('severity').notNull().default('info'),
  status: alertStatusEnum('status').notNull().default('active'),
  source: varchar('source', { length: 100 }),
  acknowledgedBy: uuid('acknowledged_by').references(() => users.id),
  acknowledgedAt: timestamp('acknowledged_at'),
  resolvedAt: timestamp('resolved_at'),
  metadata: jsonb('metadata').default({}),
  createdAt: timestamp('created_at').notNull().defaultNow(),
  updatedAt: timestamp('updated_at').notNull().defaultNow(),
});

// ─── Jobs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

export const jobs = pgTable('jobs', {
  id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  tenantId: uuid('tenant_id').notNull().references(() => tenants.id),
  name: varchar('name', { length: 255 }).notNull(),
  type: varchar('type', { length: 100 }).notNull(),
  status: jobStatusEnum('status').notNull().default('pending'),
  targetDevices: jsonb('target_devices').default([]),
  payload: jsonb('payload').default({}),
  result: jsonb('result'),
  progress: integer('progress').default(0),
  startedAt: timestamp('started_at'),
  completedAt: timestamp('completed_at'),
  scheduledAt: timestamp('scheduled_at'),
  createdBy: uuid('created_by').references(() => users.id),
  createdAt: timestamp('created_at').notNull().defaultNow(),
  updatedAt: timestamp('updated_at').notNull().defaultNow(),
});

// ─── Telemetry ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

export const telemetry = pgTable('telemetry', {
  id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  deviceId: uuid('device_id').notNull().references(() => devices.id),

  cpuUsage: real('cpu_usage'),
  memoryUsage: real('memory_usage'),
  diskUsage: real('disk_usage'),
  networkIn: real('network_in'),
  networkOut: real('network_out'),
  temperature: real('temperature'),
  batteryLevel: real('battery_level'),
  uptime: integer('uptime'),
  processCount: integer('process_count'),
  metadata: jsonb('metadata').default({}),

});

// ─── Audit Logs ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

export const auditLogs = pgTable('audit_logs', {
  id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  tenantId: uuid('tenant_id').notNull().references(() => tenants.id),

<content>
/**
 * Orchestration API Routes
 * Real device orchestration: command dispatch, bulk operations, job execution, shadow state
 */

import { FastifyInstance } from 'fastify';
import { orchestrator } from '../lib/orchestrator';
import { getWsStats } from '../lib/websocket';
import { db } from '../db/connection';
import { devices, jobs } from '../db/schema';
import { eq, and, inArray } from 'drizzle-orm';
import { operatorUp, adminOnly } from '../lib/rbac';
import { validate, commandSchema, bulkCommandSchema, shadowUpdateSchema } from '../lib/validation';

export async function orchestratorRoutes(app: FastifyInstance) {

  // ─── Orchestrator Status ────────────────────────────────────────────────

  app.get('/status', { preHandler: [app.authenticate] }, async () => {
    const stats = orchestrator.getStats();
    const wsStats = getWsStats();
    return {
      success: true,
      orchestrator: stats,
      websocket: wsStats,
      uptime: process.uptime(),
      timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
    };
  });

  // ─── Command Dispatch ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

  // Send command to a single device
  app.post('/devices/:id/command', { preHandler: [app.authenticate, operatorUp, validate(commandSchema)] }, async (request, reply) => {
    const { id } = request.params as { id: string };
    const { tenantId } = request.user;
    const body = request.body as { type: string; payload?: any; timeout?: number };

    // Verify device belongs to tenant
    const [device] = await db.select().from(devices)
      .where(and(eq(devices.id, id), eq(devices.tenantId, tenantId)))
      .limit(1);

    if (!device) return reply.status(404).send({ success: false, error: 'Device not found' });

    const command = orchestrator.dispatchCommand(
      id,
      tenantId,
      body.type as any || 'shell_execute',
      body.payload || {},
      body.timeout || 30000
    );

    return {
      success: true,
      data: {
        commandId: command.id,
        deviceId: id,
        deviceName: device.name,
        type: command.type,
        status: command.status,
        isDeviceOnline: orchestrator.isDeviceOnline(id),
        createdAt: command.createdAt,
      },
    };
  });

  // Get command status
  app.get('/commands/:commandId', { preHandler: [app.authenticate] }, async (request, reply) => {
    const { commandId } = request.params as { commandId: string };
    const command = orchestrator.getCommandStatus(commandId);

    if (!command) return reply.status(404).send({ success: false, error: 'Command not found or already completed' });

    return { success: true, data: command };
  });

  // ─── Bulk Operations ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  // Bulk command to multiple devices
  app.post('/bulk/command', { preHandler: [app.authenticate, operatorUp, validate(bulkCommandSchema)] }, async (request) => {
    const { tenantId } = request.user;
    const body = request.body as { deviceIds: string[]; type: string; payload?: any };

    const commands = await orchestrator.bulkCommand(
      body.deviceIds,
      tenantId,
      body.type as any || 'shell_execute',
      body.payload || {}
    );

    return {
      success: true,
      data: {
        dispatched: commands.length,
        commands: commands.map(c => ({
          commandId: c.id,
          deviceId: c.deviceId,
          status: c.status,
        })),
      },
    };
  });

  // Bulk reboot
  app.post('/bulk/reboot', { preHandler: [app.authenticate, adminOnly] }, async (request) => {
    const { tenantId } = request.user;
    const { deviceIds } = request.body as { deviceIds: string[] };

    const commands = await orchestrator.bulkReboot(deviceIds, tenantId);
    return {
      success: true,
      data: { dispatched: commands.length, type: 'reboot' },
    };
  });

  // Bulk update config
  app.post('/bulk/update-config', { preHandler: [app.authenticate] }, async (request) => {
    const { tenantId } = request.user;
    const { deviceIds, config } = request.body as { deviceIds: string[]; config: any };

    const commands = await orchestrator.bulkUpdateConfig(deviceIds, tenantId, config);
    return {
      success: true,
      data: { dispatched: commands.length, type: 'update_config' },
    };
  });

  // ─── Job Execution ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  // Execute a job
  app.post('/jobs/:id/execute', { preHandler: [app.authenticate] }, async (request, reply) => {
    const { id } = request.params as { id: string };

    try {
      // Execute asynchronously
      orchestrator.executeJob(id).catch(err => {
        console.error('Job execution error:', err);
      });

      return { success: true, message: 'Job execution started', jobId: id };
    } catch (err: any) {
      return reply.status(500).send({ success: false, error: err.message });
    }
  });

  // ─── Device Shadow State ────────────────────────────────────────────────

  // Get shadow state
  app.get('/devices/:id/shadow', { preHandler: [app.authenticate] }, async (request) => {
    const { id } = request.params as { id: string };
    const shadow = await orchestrator.getShadowState(id);

    return {
      success: true,
      data: {
        deviceId: id,
        ...shadow,
        isOnline: orchestrator.isDeviceOnline(id),
        lastSync: new Date().toISOString(),
      },
    };
  });

  // Update desired state
  app.put('/devices/:id/shadow', { preHandler: [app.authenticate, operatorUp, validate(shadowUpdateSchema)] }, async (request) => {
    const { id } = request.params as { id: string };
    const { desired } = request.body as { desired: Record<string, any> };

    const shadow = await orchestrator.updateDesiredState(id, desired);
    return { success: true, data: { deviceId: id, ...shadow } };
  });

  // ─── Connected Devices ──────────────────────────────────────────────────

  app.get('/connected', { preHandler: [app.authenticate] }, async () => {
    const connected = orchestrator.getConnectedDevices();
    return {
      success: true,
      data: connected,
      total: connected.length,
    };
  });

  // ─── Command History ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  app.get('/devices/:id/commands', { preHandler: [app.authenticate] }, async (request) => {
    const { id } = request.params as { id: string };
    const history = await orchestrator.getCommandHistory(id);
    return { success: true, data: history, total: history.length };
  });
}

import { FastifyInstance } from 'fastify';
import { db } from '../db/connection';
import { telemetry, devices } from '../db/schema';
import { eq, and, desc, gte, avg, sql } from 'drizzle-orm';

export async function telemetryRoutes(app: FastifyInstance) {
  // Latest telemetry per device
  app.get('/latest', { preHandler: [app.authenticate] }, async (request) => {
    const { tenantId } = request.user;

    // Get latest telemetry for each device using distinct on
    const data = await db.select().from(telemetry)
      .where(eq(telemetry.tenantId, tenantId))
      .orderBy(desc(telemetry.timestamp))
      .limit(50);

    return data;
  });

  // Aggregated telemetry stats
  app.get('/aggregated', { preHandler: [app.authenticate] }, async (request) => {
    const { tenantId } = request.user;
    const fiveMinAgo = new Date(Date.now() - 5 * 60 * 1000);

    const [result] = await db.select({
      avgCpu: avg(telemetry.cpuUsage),
      avgMemory: avg(telemetry.memoryUsage),
      avgDisk: avg(telemetry.diskUsage),
      avgTemp: avg(telemetry.temperature),
      avgNetIn: avg(telemetry.networkIn),
      avgNetOut: avg(telemetry.networkOut),
    }).from(telemetry)
      .where(and(eq(telemetry.tenantId, tenantId), gte(telemetry.timestamp, fiveMinAgo)));

    return {
      success: true,
      cpu: parseFloat(String(result?.avgCpu || 0)),
      memory: parseFloat(String(result?.avgMemory || 0)),
      disk: parseFloat(String(result?.avgDisk || 0)),
      temperature: parseFloat(String(result?.avgTemp || 0)),
      networkIn: parseFloat(String(result?.avgNetIn || 0)),
      networkOut: parseFloat(String(result?.avgNetOut || 0)),
    };
  });

  // Time series data
  app.get('/timeseries', { preHandler: [app.authenticate] }, async (request) => {
    const { tenantId } = request.user;
    const { minutes = '60' } = request.query as any;
    const since = new Date(Date.now() - parseInt(minutes) * 60 * 1000);

    const data = await db.select({
      timestamp: telemetry.timestamp,
      cpuUsage: avg(telemetry.cpuUsage),
      memoryUsage: avg(telemetry.memoryUsage),
      diskUsage: avg(telemetry.diskUsage),
      networkIn: avg(telemetry.networkIn),
      networkOut: avg(telemetry.networkOut),
    }).from(telemetry)
      .where(and(eq(telemetry.tenantId, tenantId), gte(telemetry.timestamp, since)))
      .groupBy(telemetry.timestamp)
      .orderBy(telemetry.timestamp)
      .limit(100);

    return data.map(d => ({
      timestamp: d.timestamp,
      cpu: parseFloat(String(d.cpuUsage || 0)),
      memory: parseFloat(String(d.memoryUsage || 0)),
      disk: parseFloat(String(d.diskUsage || 0)),
      networkIn: parseFloat(String(d.networkIn || 0)),
      networkOut: parseFloat(String(d.networkOut || 0)),
    }));
  });

  // Device-specific telemetry
  app.get('/device/:deviceId', { preHandler: [app.authenticate] }, async (request) => {
    const { deviceId } = request.params as { deviceId: string };
    const { limit = '50' } = request.query as any;

    const data = await db.select().from(telemetry)
      .where(eq(telemetry.deviceId, deviceId))
      .orderBy(desc(telemetry.timestamp))
      .limit(parseInt(limit));

    return data;
  });

  // Ingest telemetry (from device agents)
  app.post('/ingest', async (request) => {
    const body = request.body as any;

    const [record] = await db.insert(telemetry).values({
      deviceId: body.deviceId,
      tenantId: body.tenantId,
      cpuUsage: body.cpuUsage,
      memoryUsage: body.memoryUsage,
      diskUsage: body.diskUsage,
      networkIn: body.networkIn,
      networkOut: body.networkOut,
      temperature: body.temperature,
      batteryLevel: body.batteryLevel,
      uptime: body.uptime,
      processCount: body.processCount,
    }).returning();

    return { success: true, data: record };
  });
}

import { FastifyInstance } from 'fastify';
import { db } from '../db/connection';
import { jobs } from '../db/schema';
import { eq, count, desc } from 'drizzle-orm';

export async function jobsRoutes(app: FastifyInstance) {
  // List jobs
  app.get('/', { preHandler: [app.authenticate] }, async (request) => {
    const { tenantId } = request.user;
    const data = await db.select().from(jobs)
      .where(eq(jobs.tenantId, tenantId))
      .orderBy(desc(jobs.createdAt))
      .limit(100);
    return data;
  });

  // Job stats
  app.get('/stats', { preHandler: [app.authenticate] }, async (request) => {
    const { tenantId } = request.user;

    const result = await db.select({
      status: jobs.status,
      count: count(),
    }).from(jobs).where(eq(jobs.tenantId, tenantId)).groupBy(jobs.status);

    const stats = { total: 0, pending: 0, running: 0, completed: 0, failed: 0, cancelled: 0 };
    for (const row of result) {
      const c = Number(row.count);
      stats.total += c;
      stats[row.status as keyof typeof stats] = c;
    }

    return { success: true, ...stats };
  });

  // Create job
  app.post('/', { preHandler: [app.authenticate] }, async (request) => {
    const { tenantId, userId } = request.user;
    const body = request.body as any;

    const [job] = await db.insert(jobs).values({
      tenantId,
      name: body.name,
      type: body.type || 'custom',
      status: 'pending',
      targetDevices: body.targetDevices || [],
      payload: body.payload || {},
      createdBy: userId,
      scheduledAt: body.scheduledAt ? new Date(body.scheduledAt) : null,
    }).returning();

    return { success: true, data: job };
  });

  // Cancel job
  app.put('/:id/cancel', { preHandler: [app.authenticate] }, async (request, reply) => {
    const { id } = request.params as { id: string };
    const { tenantId } = request.user;

    const [job] = await db.update(jobs)
      .set({ status: 'cancelled', updatedAt: new Date() })
      .where(eq(jobs.id, id))
      .returning();

    if (!job) return reply.status(404).send({ success: false, error: 'Job not found' });
    return { success: true, data: job };
  });
}
